pages.full_text:
Angela + Munrose had suggested visiting Cortona, so we did, a very steep hill top town with walls, lovely, + we saw the gorgeous Fra Angelico Annunciation.

     Then tea + on to Siena. We found the campsite, from which you can see Siena on the next hill. A real armpit of a campsite – boxed in in all directions. It feels like I'm entombed/boxed in an Italian cemetery. Supper + bed.
__Sunday__ We used taxis €12 + €10 to + from Siena
[photo numbered 3: interior of building with frescoes, arched colonnade]
The Campo, where horse races take place is lovely. And the

licorice allsorts campanile + duomo are very striking. We particularly liked the frescoes, only unkovered in 1999 in the "Crypt" store room. The colours in Christ's passion are fabulous + fresh
photo_captions: The Campo, where horse races take place is lovely. And the licorice allsorts campanile + duomo are very striking. We particularly liked the frescoes, only unkovered in 1999 in the "Crypt" store room. The colours in Christ's passion are fabulous + fresh
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. The text begins mid-narrative (continuing from a previous page). 'Munrose' may be a surname; 'Angela + Munrose' appear to be friends who suggested visiting Cortona. 'unkovered' is as written (sic). The frescoes mention '1999' as the year of uncovering, giving a post-1999 date; euro prices confirm post-2002. Year cannot be determined precisely from this page alone. 'Sunday' is underlined in the original. Photo caption text runs beside and below the photo (labelled '3').

pages.notes: Handwritten page number 107 visible top-right. The page is dominated by a large postcard of Siena (aerial/elevated view of the city with the Torre del Mangia visible; printed text 'SIENA' on the postcard). The handwritten caption above the postcard reads: 'Campo / Siena / July + August / horse racing / The Palio'. The 'July + August' likely refers to the months the Palio horse race takes place in Siena, not necessarily the month of the visit.
